sources.title: What Is Kosher?
sources.description: Comprehensive overview of kosher laws and certification requirements

sources.title: Kosher Symbols
sources.description: Guide to reliable kosher certification symbols and warning about plain 'K' labels

sources.title: The Kosher Symbols Clarified
sources.description: Detailed explanation of OK symbol restrictions and kosher categories

sources.title: Orthodox Union Kosher
sources.description: History of the OU kosher certification, the world's largest

sources.title: About the OU
sources.description: OU certifies 1.3 million products in 15,000 plants across 105 countries

sources.title: Star-K
sources.description: Information about Star-K certification and Star-S for Passover kitniyot

sources.title: Kosher Symbols
sources.description: Database of kosher symbols approved by Rabbi Eidlitz

key_certification_agencies.name: OK Kosher Certification
key_certification_agencies.notes: Clear symbol restriction system (D, DE, M, P, PY, F, CY)

key_certification_agencies.name: Star-K
key_certification_agencies.notes: Known for stricter standards (e.g., Cholov Yisroel milk requirements)

key_certification_agencies.name: Kof-K
key_certification_agencies.notes: One of the 'Big Four' US certification agencies

key_certification_agencies.name: cRc (Chicago Rabbinical Council)
key_certification_agencies.notes: Serves Midwest region of United States

chinese_parallels.parallel: Similar to China's food safety certification system (食品安全认证), kosher symbols provide consumer confidence
chinese_parallels.dynasty_context: Ancient Chinese dynasties had imperial food inspectors - kosher supervision is a 3,000+ year tradition

chinese_parallels.parallel: Like China's CCC (China Compulsory Certificate) mark, kosher symbols show independent verification
chinese_parallels.notes: Both systems prevent fraud and protect consumers
